summary: Frontend-only review of Automatik Media premium landing page. All 9 sections render, all 51 required data-testids are present, hero video loads, navbar glassmorphism activates on scroll, smooth-scroll from 'Ver Cómo Funciona' to #proceso works, testimonials use initials-only avatars (no ), mobile menu opens at 390px with no horizontal overflow, and no console errors. Calendly CTAs (hero, navbar, final, footer) all correctly open https://calendly.com/cheloxnz/30min in a new tab. The only real issue: the floating WhatsApp button is visually overlapped/click-intercepted by the Emergent preview badge in the bottom-right corner.

ui_bugs.issue: The floating WhatsApp button (fixed bottom-6 right-6 z-40) is overlapped by the Emergent preview badge (#emergent-badge, fixed bottom:16px right:16px z-index:9999, ~178x40px). On the preview URL the badge intercepts clicks — clicking the WhatsApp float opens https://app.emergent.sh/?utm_source=emergent-badge instead of wa.me. The button is also partially hidden behind the badge visually at the bottom-right corner.

action_items: Reposition WhatsAppFloat so it does not collide with the Emergent badge in the bottom-right. Recommended fix in /app/frontend/src/components/WhatsAppFloat.jsx: change the wrapper class from 'fixed bottom-6 right-6 z-40' to something like 'fixed bottom-24 right-6 z-50' (move it ~80–96px up so it clears the 40px Emergent badge), OR move it to 'bottom-6 left-6'. Also bump z-index above 9999 only if needed, but vertical offset is the cleaner fix.
critical_code_review_comments: Hero video has autoPlay + muted=!playing. On first render playing=false so muted=true and the video autoplays muted; clicking the play overlay correctly unmutes and toggles. Looks fine but consider showing the play overlay only when paused (currently overlay always covers the video, which means the video plays underneath but the play icon remains on top — minor UX inconsistency).
WhatsAppFloat z-index (z-40) is below the Emergent badge (z-9999). On preview/staging this badge will always exist — the float should be placed to coexist with it.
All external CTAs correctly use window.open(url, '_blank', 'noopener,noreferrer') — good security hygiene.
All required data-testid attributes are present and consistent.
No backend integration in this landing (per spec) — verified no /api calls fired.

success_rate.frontend: 97% (1 click-interception bug on whatsapp-float; all other 50+ checks pass)

test_credentials: N/A — landing page has no auth
context_for_next_testing_agent: All sections, data-testids, CTAs, scroll behavior, mobile responsiveness, and console were verified via Playwright. The only outstanding issue is the WhatsAppFloat being overlapped by the Emergent badge. Note for future verification: the wa.me/5491168754798 URL correctly redirects through to https://api.whatsapp.com/send/?phone=5491168754798&text=... — this is WhatsApp's own behavior, not a bug. Validate the float fix by clicking the floating button (data-testid='whatsapp-float') and asserting the new tab URL contains 'wa.me' or 'api.whatsapp.com/send' AND phone=5491168754798.
rca of the issue: Root cause: The Emergent preview badge anchor (#emergent-badge) is injected into /app/frontend/public/index.html with inline styles 'position: fixed; bottom: 16px; right: 16px; z-index: 9999'. The WhatsAppFloat container is rendered with 'fixed bottom-6 (=24px) right-6 (=24px) z-40'. Because the badge has a much higher z-index (9999 > 40) and sits at the same screen corner, the badge sits on top of and intercepts pointer events for the WhatsApp button. Reproduction: load the preview URL, click the green circular WhatsApp icon at bottom-right; tab that opens is app.emergent.sh instead of wa.me. Mitigation: in WhatsAppFloat.jsx change wrapper className from 'fixed bottom-6 right-6 z-40' to 'fixed bottom-24 right-6 z-50' (or bottom-20). This places the WhatsApp button visually above the badge and removes the overlap. The Emergent badge (40px tall + 16px bottom = bottom edge consumes the 0–56px band on the right) — bottom-24 (=96px) provides safe clearance.
